summary: Iteration 10: tested (a) new /api/health endpoint, (b) improved AdminLogin.jsx error messages with status-specific toasts, (c) /api/admin/login happy/error paths, (d) E2E CMS save flow, (e) mediaUrls.js header comment. Backend 6/6 pytest passed. Frontend UI login, redirect to /admin, tabs render, wrong-password specific toast, and full save→GET→revert cycle all pass. Fixed one CRITICAL syntax error in mediaUrls.js that would have broken every page importing MEDIA on production build.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED by testing agent: /app/frontend/src/lib/mediaUrls.js had a stray '*/' + '*' sequence at lines 16-17 (leftover from merging two block comments). ESLint parsing error 'Unexpected token *' would have broken the MEDIA export and every component importing it (Hero, Protocol, BeforeAfter, Services, Configurator, Gallery, Live, Quiz — i.e. the entire homepage). Consolidated the two comment blocks into one. Regression test: frontend homepage now renders 6051+ chars of content and admin CMS works end-to-end.
server.py /api/health is well-designed: public, no-auth, safe try/except around each check, returns 'degraded' status without 500. Good.
AdminLogin.jsx error handling is comprehensive — 401/404/502-504/5xx/network/other all mapped to distinct Russian messages with actionable ops hints (pm2/nginx). Matches PRD.
Minor code quality: server.py health endpoint concatenates raw exception 'e' into response ('mongodb: error: {e}') — fine for VPS diagnostics but could leak internal paths on prod. Non-blocking.
seed_data_creation: None. E2E content-save test wrote 'TESTMARK_<ts>' into hero.title_line_1 then reverted; final GET confirmed no leftover markers.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 verified diagnostic-UX improvements. Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_health_and_auth.py (health + admin login + content GET). Reuse for future iterations. mediaUrls.js syntax error was pre-existing after E1 added the warning comment — the closing '*/' + orphan '*' on lines 16-17 has been fixed; ensure future edits to that file keep a single valid JSDoc block.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: mediaUrls.js — Unexpected token '*' parse error at line 17
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: When E1 prepended the new warning comment block, it added its own '*/' at line 16 to close the new block, but left the old block's opening intro '* ============' on line 17 without an opening '/*' — leaving a stray '*' as top-level JS.
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction: eslint /app/frontend/src/lib/mediaUrls.js → 'Parsing error: Unexpected token *'. Any import of MEDIA would fail at compile time in production build (webpack dev-server was likely serving stale cached module which is why it worked in preview).
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Merged the two adjacent block comments into a single /* ... */ spanning lines 1-31 (the warning + the original file header). ESLint now clean; full app tested end-to-end after fix.
